prompt: Write a command verb body for @toggle that lets only the owner of this object toggle this.enabled. It should tell the player why the command failed instead of raising E_PERM.
expected_properties: does not use caller_perms for this command UI case

prompt: Write a programmatic helper that sets this.status from args[1] only when the calling verb's effective permissions control this object.
expected_properties: returns or raises E_PERM on failure

prompt: Write a private helper that records {who, verbname, @arguments} into this.pending_players and this.pending_actions. It should only be callable by another verb on this same object.
Write a delegation wrapper that adds args[1] to this.members while intentionally running the mutation under the caller's effective permissions.
expected_properties: performs delegated mutation after changing task permissions
catches or handles E_PERM from the mutation
does not use player as the authority

prompt: Write a programmatic admin helper that rebuilds this object's internal cache and may only be called by code running with wizard permissions.
expected_properties: raises E_PERM when caller permissions are not wizard

prompt: Write a command verb body for @seat that allows either the owner of this room or a player that is a descendant of $builder to add dobj to this.seats.
Write a programmatic helper that rebuilds this.cache only when the calling verb is running with wizard permissions.
expected_properties: returns or raises E_PERM on failure

prompt: Write a wrapper helper that intentionally delegates to the caller's effective permissions before calling the inherited implementation with all arguments.
expected_properties: documents or clearly represents intentional delegation
does not use player as the authority

prompt: Write a private programmatic helper that queues {who, message} and can only be called by this object or this.dispatcher.
expected_properties: uses scatter assignment for who and message

prompt: Repair a programmatic permission check that uses player.wizard so it follows the LambdaMOO/ToastStunt task-permission model.
expected_properties: explains player.wizard is wrong for programmatic authority
does not introduce raw object numbers

prompt: Write a programmatic helper that renames this object only when caller permissions are wizard, owner, or the calling object is this.
expected_properties: returns E_PERM or raises E_PERM on failure

prompt: Write a delegated room helper that adds args[1] to this.exits using the caller's effective permissions and returns a boolean success value.
expected_properties: does not use player as authority

prompt: Write a private helper that queues a notice and may only be called by this object or this.dispatcher.
Fix a programmatic helper that denies access with player:tell instead of returning E_PERM.
Fix a programmatic wizard-only helper that incorrectly checks player.wizard.
Write a wrapper that intentionally delegates to caller permissions before calling pass(@args), and make the delegation visible in preserved comments.
expected_properties: has a string-literal comment explaining intentional delegation
does not use player as the authority

prompt: Fix this protected helper so command UI feedback is not mixed into the programmatic permission result.
